title: Harness Diagnostic Matrix
description: Diagnosis-only schema for any harness (eval, clinical nutrition, LLM benchmark, code-test, data pipeline, product demo, etc.). It maps symptom -> system site -> evidence -> likely cause/problem hypothesis -> differentials -> next minimal evidence. It deliberately has NO field for fixes, implementation plans, PR sequences, or action cards. Every cause/problem hypothesis must carry an evidence pointer; if evidence is weak, mark insufficient_evidence=true and do not guess.

harness.description: Name/kind of the harness under diagnosis.

title.description: Optional display title for the matrix.

scope.description: Evidence scope / window actually examined (so readers know what was NOT read).

footer.description: Optional boundary note shown at the bottom of the rendered matrix.

rows.description: One row per symptom/complaint being diagnosed.

symptom.description: Complaint / observed symptom in plain language.

site.description: Affected system node(s) / location(s) inside the harness.

evidence.description: Evidence pointer(s): log id, wake/turn index, file:line, metric, error head/tail, config diff, user-supplied snippet. REQUIRED to support any hypothesis.

hypothesis.description: Likely cause / problem hypothesis. NOT a fix. Must be backed by the evidence pointer, or the row must set insufficient_evidence=true.

differentials.description: Competing / alternative causes that the current evidence cannot yet rule out.

next_evidence.description: Next minimal evidence to collect or question to answer to discriminate the hypotheses. This is a DIAGNOSTIC step, never a repair step.

confidence.description: Confidence in the leading hypothesis. Use insufficient/unknown when evidence is weak.

insufficient_evidence.description: True when evidence does not yet support any single cause; renders the confidence cell as 'insufficient'.
